top_findings.title: Partial PUT /books/{id} updates fail at runtime for non-prefix field subsets
top_findings.evidence: src/lib.rs:228-263 — hardcodes placeholders (title=?1, author=?2, year=?3, isbn=?4) but binds params positionally via params_from_iter over only the present fields, so a body that omits an earlier field (e.g. {"author":"X"} -> query references ?2 with only one supplied param bound to ?1) leaves a placeholder unbound and rusqlite errors -> 500. Only full updates or a leading prefix (title / title+author / ...) work. README:95 promises 'All fields are optional'.
top_findings.suggestion: Build the SET clause and the parameter list together with sequential placeholders (?1, ?2, ... in push order) instead of fixed field-indexed placeholders.
